T1566.001.name: Spearphishing Attachment Execution
T1566.001.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND Image='*\cmd.exe' OR Image='*\powershell.exe'
T1566.001.description: Detects when Outlook spawns cmd.exe or powershell.exe, indicating macro-based spearphishing attachment execution.
T1566.001.author: SigmaHQ

T1566.001.name: Suspicious Office Document Download
T1566.001.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Suspicious Office File Download"; fileext:"docm"; sid:1000001;)
T1566.001.description: Detects download of macro-enabled Office documents (.docm) from external networks via HTTP.
T1566.001.author: Emerging Threats

T1566.002.name: Spearphishing Link Clicked
T1566.002.query: EventID=1 AND ParentImage='*\OUTLOOK.EXE' AND Image='*\chrome.exe' OR Image='*\msedge.exe'
T1566.002.description: Detects when Outlook spawns a browser, suggesting the user clicked a spearphishing link in an email.
T1566.002.author: SigmaHQ

T1059.001.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution
T1059.001.query: EventID=1 AND (CommandLine contains '-enc' OR CommandLine contains 'DownloadString')
T1059.001.description: Detects PowerShell execution with encoded commands (-enc/-encodedcommand) or DownloadString, common indicators of malicious script delivery.
T1059.001.author: SigmaHQ

T1059.001.name: PowerShell Script Block Logging
T1059.001.description: Wazuh rule to detect malicious PowerShell script block content including Invoke-Mimikatz and DownloadString via Windows Event ID 4104.
T1059.001.author: Wazuh

T1059.001.name: PowerShell Network Connection
T1059.001.description: Detects PowerShell making outbound network connections, which may indicate C2 communication, download cradles, or data exfiltration.
T1059.001.author: Microsoft Sysmon

T1003.001.name: LSASS Memory Dump
T1003.001.description: Detects suspicious access to LSASS process memory with the 0x1010 access flag used by Mimikatz and similar tools to dump credentials.
T1003.001.author: SigmaHQ

T1003.001.name: Credential Dumping via Mimikatz
T1003.001.description: Detects direct execution of mimikatz.exe. Highly suspicious — Mimikatz is exclusively used for credential theft and post-exploitation.
T1003.001.author: Elastic

T1053.005.name: Scheduled Task Creation
T1053.005.query: EventID=1 AND Image='*\schtasks.exe' AND CommandLine contains '/create'
T1053.005.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe. Used for persistence and sometimes lateral movement.
T1053.005.author: SigmaHQ

T1053.005.name: Scheduled Task Registry Modification
T1053.005.description: Detects modifications to the Windows Task Scheduler registry hive at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache.
T1053.005.author: Wazuh

T1190.name: Log4j JNDI Lookup Execution
T1190.query: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Log4j JNDI Attempt"; content:"${jndi:"; sid:1000002;)
T1190.description: Detects CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) exploitation attempts via JNDI injection pattern in HTTP requests.
T1190.author: Emerging Threats

T1190.name: Web Server Exploitation
T1190.description: Detects Log4j JNDI LDAP exploitation pattern in web server logs via Wazuh log analysis.
T1190.author: Wazuh

T1505.003.name: Webshell Creation
T1505.003.description: Detects creation of .aspx files by the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe), a strong indicator of webshell deployment.
T1505.003.author: SigmaHQ

T1068.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
T1068.description: Detects cmd.exe spawned by spoolsv.exe, a classic indicator of Print Spooler exploitation (e.g., PrintNightmare CVE-2021-1675).
T1068.author: SigmaHQ

T1071.004.name: DNS Tunneling Payload
T1071.004.query: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 53 (msg:"Suspicious Long DNS Query"; dsize:>100; sid:1000003;)
T1071.004.description: Detects unusually long DNS queries (>100 bytes) that may indicate DNS tunneling for C2 communication or data exfiltration.
T1071.004.author: Emerging Threats

T1550.002.name: Pass the Hash Activity
T1550.002.description: Detects Pass-the-Hash (PtH) attacks by identifying NewCredentials logon (Type 9) with Negotiate authentication package, characteristic of PtH using stolen NTLM hashes.
T1550.002.author: SigmaHQ

T1055.001.name: Process Injection - Dynamic-link Library Injection
T1055.001.description: Detects CreateRemoteThread calls targeting svchost.exe (Sysmon Event ID 8), a common DLL injection technique for code execution in the context of a trusted system process.
T1055.001.author: SigmaHQ

T1070.001.name: Windows Event Log Cleared
T1070.001.description: Detects clearing of Windows Security (1102) or System (104) event logs, a common attacker technique to destroy forensic evidence.
T1070.001.author: SigmaHQ

T1218.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
T1218.description: Detects execution of signed Windows binaries (LOLBins) like rundll32.exe and regsvr32.exe that can proxy malicious code execution to bypass application whitelisting.
T1218.author: SigmaHQ
